titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/142

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=415 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:07:40.982791+00 | translation moa=416 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:07:42.590974+00

span.cl:
Omnibus hoc scriptum uisuris uel audituris Walterus filius Alani Domini Regis scocie senescallus salutem
in domino .

Nouerit vniuersitas uestra quod terram de Molle cum rectis diuisis suis et quicquid continetur imfra
easdem diuisas quam tenui in feodo de domino Willelmo de vesci dedi in escambium Monachis de Melros pro quadam
terram que dicitur Freretun’ cum omnibus pertinenciis suis . que omnia Moniales de subereuuic ibidem possederunt .
et insuper cum eadem terra que dicitur freretun  dederunt mihi idem monachi racione dicti escambii ducentas
marcas . Quam terram de Molle cum contentis imfra diuisas suas et pertinenciis suis  ego et heredes mei eisdem –
monachis in perpetuum contra omnes Warantizabimus . defendemus et manutenebimus . In cuius rei testimonium presens
scriptum sigilli mei apposicione roboraui .

Hiis testibus . Thomas Croc . Symone filio bertulfi . Ada le Waleys .
Willelmo de hauekerill’ . Roberto de kiphau . militibus . Willelmo et Roberto capellanus . nostris . Leonio de Normannuil’ . Ricardo de Muskilbur’ . Iohanne de Lycest’ Monachis de Melros et multis aliis .

p.cl: To all who shall see or hear this written deed, Walter son of Alan, steward of the lord king of Scotland, sends greeting in the Lord.

Let your whole community know that the land of Mow with its correct boundaries and with whatever is contained within the same boundaries that I held in feu of the lord William de Vescy I have given in exchange to the monks of Melrose in return for a land that is called ‘Freretun’, with all its pertinents, everything that the nuns of South Berwick possessed in that place; and in addition, together with the same land which is called ‘Freretun’, the same monks have given me two hundred merks by reason of the same exchange. I and my heirs shall warrant, defend and maintain this land of Mow with its contents and its pertinents within its boundaries. As witness of this matter I have reinforced the present written deed by the application of my seal.

With these witnesses: Thomas Crook, Simon son of Bertolf, Adam Wallace, William of Halkerston, Robert of Kippo, knights; William and Robert, our chaplains; Leon de Normanville, Richard of Musselburgh, John of Leicester, monks of Melrose; and many others.
